key_points: Water scarcity is caused by population growth, industrialization, and urbanization
Multipurpose river projects provide irrigation, electricity, flood control, and water supply
Traditional water harvesting systems were designed according to local climate
Groundwater depletion occurs due to excessive extraction
Urban areas need rainwater harvesting to recharge groundwater
examples: Hirakud dam is built on the Mahanadi river
Sardar Sarovar project is built on the Narmada river
Johads are used in Rajasthan for rainwater storage
Kuls are irrigation channels used in the Western Himalayas
Tamil Nadu made rooftop rainwater harvesting compulsory
formulas_or_concepts: Multipurpose River Project: Dam used for irrigation, power, and flood control
Groundwater Recharge: Water entering the soil and replenishing aquifers
Water Conservation: Careful use and protection of water resources
exam_tips: Explain advantages and disadvantages of dams
Link causes of water scarcity with human activities
Use diagrams to explain rainwater harvesting
